sentences.base_language: My name is Audrey, I'm from Philadelphia or something
sentences.target_language: Mi nombre es Audrey, soy de Filadelfia o algo así
sentences.grammarNote:
**Mi nombre es** = my name is — more formal than **Me llamo** (I call myself), which is more conversational.
**Soy de** + place = I'm from: **soy de Madrid**, **soy de México**, **soy de Filadelfia**.
**O algo así** = or something like that — a common filler phrase.

sentences.base_language: I have a lot of hobbies!
sentences.grammarNote:
**Tengo** = I have — **tener** is irregular: **tengo** (not *teno*). Full present: **tengo, tienes, tiene, tenemos, tenéis, tienen**.
**Muchos** = many/a lot of — agrees with the masculine plural noun: **muchos pasatiempos** (many pastimes), **muchas ideas** (many ideas).
**Pasatiempo** = pastime — from **pasar** (to pass) + **tiempo** (time).

sentences.base_language: I like to ride bikes with my boyfriend Waller
sentences.target_language: Me gusta andar en bicicleta con mi novio Waller
sentences.grammarNote:
**Andar en bicicleta** = to ride a bike — literally "to go by bicycle".
Also common: **montar en bicicleta** (especially Spain), **ir en bici** (informal).
**Me gusta + infinitive** — **andar** is the infinitive here.
**Mi novio** = my boyfriend — **novio/novia** covers boyfriend/girlfriend and fiancé(e); context clarifies.

sentences.base_language: I like to make crafts too
sentences.grammarNote:
**Manualidades** = handicrafts/DIY crafts — from **mano** (hand) + **-ualidades**: literally "hand-works".
Also: **artesanías** (crafts, with more of an artisan/traditional art connotation).
**También** = also/too — an adverb placed at the start or before the verb.
**Hacer manualidades** = to do/make crafts — **hacer** is the go-to verb for activities.

sentences.base_language: What kind of crafts you might ask
sentences.target_language: Quizás te preguntes qué tipo de manualidades
sentences.grammarNote: "Te preguntes" is the present subjunctive, used after 'quizás' to express something uncertain or hypothetical.

sentences.base_language: Well the person writing this didn't ask so I'm guessing puppets
sentences.target_language: Bueno, la persona que escribe esto no preguntó, así que supongo que títeres
sentences.grammarNote:
**La persona que escribe** = the person who writes — **que** introduces a relative clause describing **la persona**.
**Preguntó** = asked — preterite of **preguntar** (regular -ar verb): **pregunté, preguntaste, preguntó...**
**Supongo** = I suppose/guess — from **suponer** (irregular yo: **supongo**).
**Títeres** = puppets (plural) — **el títere** (puppet/marionette).

sentences.base_language: I also have a lot of plants
sentences.grammarNote:
**Muchas plantas** — **muchas** (feminine plural) agrees with **plantas** (feminine).
Contrast with the earlier **muchos pasatiempos** (masculine plural).
**Las plantas** = plants — **la planta** is feminine: **la planta**, **las plantas**.

sentences.base_language: Like more plants than you'd ever imagine
sentences.target_language: Como más plantas de las que te puedas imaginar
sentences.grammarNote:
**Más... de las que** = more... than (with a quantity comparison involving a plural feminine noun).
Comparison with clauses: **más de lo que** (more than what — with uncountable/abstract), **más de los que / de las que** (more than — with countable plural nouns).
**Te puedas imaginar** — subjunctive in the relative clause because it refers to an undefined/hypothetical quantity.

sentences.base_language: I work as a fortune teller
sentences.grammarNote:
**Trabajo como adivina** — **trabajar como** + profession (no article).
**Adivina** = fortune teller/seer (feminine) — **adivino / adivina**. From **adivinar** (to guess/divine/predict).
Note: **ser** would also work: **soy adivina** — but **trabajo como** emphasizes the professional role.

sentences.base_language: I can read tarot cards and predict the future
sentences.target_language: Puedo leer las cartas del tarot y predecir el futuro
sentences.grammarNote:
**Puedo** = I can — **poder** (o→ue): **puedo, puedes, puede...**
**Las cartas del tarot** = tarot cards — **del** = de + el (mandatory contraction).
**Predecir** = to predict — an irregular verb related to **decir** (to say): **predigo, predices, predice...**
**El futuro** = the future — definite article used when talking about a specific or abstract concept.

sentences.base_language: I play dungeons and dragons with my best friends
sentences.target_language: Juego a Dragones y Mazmorras con mis mejores amigos
sentences.grammarNote:
**Juego a** + game — **jugar a** is the standard construction for games: **juego al fútbol**, **juego al ajedrez**, **juego a Dragones y Mazmorras**.
Stem-changing verb: **jugar** (u→ue): **juego, juegas, juega, jugamos, jugáis, juegan**.
**Mis mejores amigos** = my best friends — **mejor** (better/best) is invariable for gender: **el mejor / la mejor**.

sentences.base_language: Other than that I am pretty chill
sentences.target_language: Aparte de eso, soy bastante tranquila
sentences.grammarNote:
**Aparte de eso** = other than that / apart from that — a discourse connector.
**Soy tranquila** — **ser** for personality traits: **tranquila** (calm/chill) is a permanent character description.
**Bastante** = quite/pretty/enough — an adverb that intensifies the adjective: **bastante tranquila** (quite calm), **bastante interesante** (pretty interesting).
**Tranquila** agrees with the speaker (feminine): **tranquilo** for masculine.

**Compro** = I buy — **comprar** is a regular -ar verb.
**A veces** = sometimes — a frequency adverb. Compare: **siempre** (always), **nunca** (never), **a menudo** (often), **de vez en cuando** (from time to time).
**Bagels** is an anglicism — used directly in Spanish without modification.

sentences.base_language: One day Jeff will ask more follow up questions to write more on this story
sentences.target_language: Un día Jeff hará más preguntas de seguimiento para escribir más sobre esta historia
sentences.grammarNote:
**Hará** = will ask/make — future tense of **hacer**: **haré, harás, hará, haremos, haréis, harán**.
**Hacer preguntas** = to ask questions (literally "to make questions") — don't use *preguntar preguntas*.
**Para + infinitive** = in order to: **para escribir más** (in order to write more).
**Esta historia** = this story — **esta** (feminine demonstrative) agrees with **historia** (feminine).

sentences.base_language: But for now, that is about it
sentences.target_language: Pero por ahora, eso es todo
sentences.grammarNote:
**Por ahora** = for now — a time expression. Compare: **ahora mismo** (right now), **de momento** (for the moment).
**Eso es todo** = that's all/that's it — **eso** is the neuter demonstrative (used when gender is unspecified or referring to a general idea).
**Pero** = but (contrast); compare with **sino** (but rather — used after a negation): **No trabajo, sino estudio** (I don't work, but rather I study).
